LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT JB MAINTENANCE'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AGAINST PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT. THE COURT STATES THAT THE PLAINTIFF FILED A NOTICE OF NON-OPPOSITION AND THAT THE CO-DEFENDANTS OPPOSE THE TENTATIVE RULING. THE COURT INQUIRED ABOUT THE CO-DEFENANTS STANDING TO DO SO. AT THE CONCLUSION OF THE DISCUSSIONS/ARGUMENT, THE COURT CONTINUES DEFENDANT JB MAINTENANCE'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AGAINST PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT TO FRIDAY, MAY 21, 2021 AT 9:30 A.M., IN DEPT. 302. ALL COUNSEL ARE INVITED TO FILE A FIVE-PAGE SUPPLEMENTAL BRIEF ADDRESSING THIS ISSUE. THE SUPPLEMENTAL BRIEFS ARE TO BE ON FILE BY FRIDAY, MAY 14, 2021. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER; CLERK: JACQUELINE ALAMEDA; COURT REPORTER: ROSE MILLER, CSR NO. 5041, EMAIL:RMILLERCSR@GMAIL.COM. (302/RBU)

MOTION TO CONTINUE JURY TRIAL
Off Calendar May-27-2021 Text Ruling

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment By Defendant Jb Maintenance Against Plaintiffs Complaint
Off Calendar May-21-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances (via ZOOM videoconference):
Atty. Brenden M. Norton, Esq. Of Bryman & Apelian, 818.225.5151, for the Plaintiff Samira Kharrubi;
Atty. Terry M. Finck, Esq. of De La Pena & Holiday LLP, 415.268.8000, for Defendants Columbus Residence LLC, Charybdis LLC;
Atty. Carla J. Gagne, Esq. of Walker & Kirkpatrick, 818.334.5380, for Defendant JB Maintenance.

DEFENDANT JB MAINTENANCE'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Against Plaintiff's Complaint.

The matter is called. ARGUMENT is held. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendant JB Maintenance's summary judgment motion is granted.

Plaintiff sued after slipping and falling on water at an apartment building. Defendant Charybdis, LLC leases the property to defendant Columbus Residence LLC, which contracted with JB for cleaning services. The contract calls for JB to indemnify Columbus for JB's negligence, but no party filed a cross-claim.

JB moved for summary judgment on plaintiff's complaint. Plaintiff filed a non-opposition; she says facts learned in discovery make her claim against JB unviable. However, Columbus and Charybdis filed a "partial opposition" to JB's summary judgment motion. Whether they had standing to do so was not addressed in the papers, so I asked for, and received, supplemental briefing.

Based on that briefing, no citable California state court decision directly addresses opposition by a defendant with no cross-claim to another defendant's summary judgment motion. Atlas Construction Supply, Inc. v. Swinerton Builders (2021) 274 Cal.Rptr. 414 was recently depublished. Columbus Line, Inc. v. Gray Line Sight-Seeing Companies Associated, Inc. (1981) 120 Cal.App.3d 622 had cross-claims.

However, a decision by a California federal court holds that a co-defendant without a cross-claim lacks standing to oppose a summary judgment motion. (Eckert v. City of Sacramento (2009) 2009 WL 3211278; see also Fraioli v. Lemcke (D.R.I. 2004) 328 F.Supp.2d 250, 263; Blonder v. Casco Inn Residential Care, Inc. (D. Me. 2000) WL 761895.) I am persuaded by the logic of these decisions.

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda; Court Reporter: Rose Miller, CSR No. 5041, Email: RMillerCSR@gmail.com. (302/RBU)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment By Defendant Jb Maintenance Against Plaintiffs Complaint
Off Calendar May-11-2021 Continued To May-21-21 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances (via ZOOM videoconferenc):
Atty. Brenden Michael Norton, Esq. of Bryman & Apelin, 818.225.5151, appearing for the Plaintiff;
Atty. Terry Finck, Esq. of De La Pena & Holiday LLP, 415.268.8000, appearing for the Defendants Columbus Residence LLC and Charybdis;
Atty Carla Cagne, Esq. of Walker Kirkpatrick, 818.334.5380, appearing for Defendant JB Maintenance.

DEFENDANT JB MAINTENANCE'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AGAINST PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT.

The matter is called. The Court states that the Plaintiff filed a notice of non-opposition and that the Co-Defendants oppose the tentative ruling. The Court inquired about the Co-Defendants standing.

DISCUSSIONS/ARGUMENT on the standing issue related to the Co-Defendants are held. Mr. Finck argues in support of his client's standing and addresses CCP 437(c)(l).

At the conclusion of the discussions/argument, the Court continues DEFENDANT JB MAINTENANCE'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AGAINST PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT to Friday, May 21, 2021 at 9:30 a.m., in Dept. 302.

All counsel are invited to file a five-page supplemental brief addressing this issue. The supplemental briefs are to be on file by Friday, May 14, 2021.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda; Court Reporter: Rose Miller, CSR No. 5041, Email:RMillerCSR@gmail.com. (302/RBU)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Defs. Motion For Protective Order
Off Calendar Mar-25-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: (via ZOOM videoconference):
Atty. Brendan Norton, Esq. of Bryman & Apelin, 925.518.7470, appearing for the Plaintiff;
Atty. Terry Finck, Esq. of De La Pena & Holiday LLP, appearing for Defendants Columbus Residence LLC and Charybdis;
Atty. Carla Gagne, Esq. of Walker Kirkpatrick, 818.334.5380, appearing for Defendant JV Maintenance.

DEFENDANTS COLUMBUS RESIDENCE, LLC, CHARYBDIS, LLC'S Motion For Protective Order.

Matter called, DISCUSSIONS are held.

Counsel inform the Court that they are appearing to seek the Court's approval of the stipulation/informal agreement that they have reached and submitted to the Court. Mr. Finck stated that they need the protective order, that they are also requesting that the Court retain jurisdiction over the sanctions, and that it is their request that if any issues were to come up that they wish to bring the matter back before the Court without further briefing.

The Court stated that it was glad that the parties reached an agreement and that it would sign the order attached to the stipulation.

The Order on Stipulation Regarding Attorney Conduct at Depositions is signed by the Court.
(see order on stipulation signed this date for full details)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, Email: maria.torreano@gmail.com. (302/EPS)
